section.title: Open questions
section.intro: Unresolved problems: environmental cost, deployment in low-resource settings, transparency incentives, and whether AI is the right tool for the problem.

resources.title: The Transparency Paradox in AI Disclosure
resources.source: Risk Management and Healthcare Policy (Dove Medical Press)
resources.description: Researchers avoid disclosing AI use because disclosure marks work as suspect, while non-disclosure carries no penalty. Transparency policies exist but the incentive structure punishes compliance.

resources.title: Selective Non-Transparency in AI Use
resources.description: Argues blanket AI disclosure requirements convert process knowledge into surveillance. Knowing exactly how a researcher used AI changes how peers evaluate the work – not always productively.

resources.title: Deploying AI in LMICs: Is AI Even the Right Answer?
resources.description: In many LMIC health contexts, the binding constraint is infrastructure, staffing, or supply chain – not information processing. Asks whether AI addresses the actual bottleneck.

resources.title: AI's Hidden Carbon and Water Footprint
resources.description: Updated environmental impact data with calls for mandatory environmental reporting for AI deployments.

resources.title: 2026 Global Health Trends
resources.description: Places AI adoption in global health alongside climate migration, shifting funder priorities, and erosion of multilateral coordination.

resources.title: Deploying Medical AI in Low-Resource Settings: A Scoping Review
resources.description: Scoping review of AI health tools deployed in low-resource clinical settings. Performance degrades when infrastructure, data quality, and workflow conditions differ from the development context.
